medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Ataxic-spastic signs evident from the age of 15 years. Clinical evaluation before starting DHA supplementation (T0, 39 years) showed moderate spastic-ataxic gait, mild dysarthria, mild diffuse leg weakness and dysmetria, bilateral Babinski sign, pes cavus and hammertoes.
medical_history.family_history: Neurologically-healthy father had died at the age of 40 of myocardial infarction linked to severe hypercholesterolemia.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Moderate spastic-ataxic gait, mild dysarthria, mild diffuse leg weakness and dysmetria, bilateral Babinski sign, pes cavus and hammertoes. Electroneurography showed severe sensorimotor axonal and demyelinating polyneuropathy. Brain MRI documented mild cerebellar atrophy, prominent in the cerebellar vermis, and hypointense stripes in the pons. Spine MRI was normal.

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Serum low-density lipoprotein (LDL) and total cholesterol levels
laboratory_examinations.date: Before treatment (T0) and after 20 months of treatment
laboratory_examinations.findings: P1: LDL at T0 was 177 mg/dL (normal < 100), after 20 months was 241 mg/dL; total cholesterol at T0 was 241 mg/dL (normal < 200), after 20 months was 282 mg/dL. HDL cholesterol at T0 was 50 mg/dL (normal > 40), after 20 months was 30 mg/dL. Triglycerides at T0 were 62 mg/dL (normal < 150), after 20 months were 54 mg/dL.
laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: LDL and total cholesterol levels increased over time; HDL cholesterol decreased; triglycerides remained within normal range.

radiographic_examinations.findings: P1: Mild cerebellar atrophy, prominent in the cerebellar vermis, and hypointense stripes in the pons. P2: Slight atrophy of the superior cerebellar vermis, corpus callosum thinning, and cervical spinal cord atrophy.
P1: Normal. P2: Cervical spinal cord atrophy.

other_tests.findings: P1: Severe sensorimotor axonal and demyelinating polyneuropathy. P2: Sensorimotor axonal polyneuropathy.

other_tests.date: Before treatment (T0) and after 6, 12, and 20 months of treatment
other_tests.changes_over_time: P1: Initial increase, then decrease. P2: Initial decrease, then stabilization.

other_tests.procedure_name: Scale for the Assessment and Rating of Ataxia (SARA)
other_tests.date: Before treatment (T0) and after 6, 12, and 20 months of treatment
other_tests.changes_over_time: P1: Initial increase, then decrease. P2: Initial decrease, then increase, then decrease.

other_tests.procedure_name: Disease-specific severity index for adults with autosomal recessive spastic ataxia of Charlevoix-Saguenay (DSI-ARSACS)
other_tests.date: Before treatment (T0) and after 6, 12, and 20 months of treatment
other_tests.changes_over_time: P1: Initial decrease, then increase. P2: Initial increase, then decrease, then increase.

other_tests.date: Before treatment (T0) and after 6, 12, and 20 months of treatment
other_tests.changes_over_time: P1: Initial improvement, then slight deterioration. P2: Initial improvement, then slight deterioration.

other_tests.date: Before treatment (T0) and after 6, 12, and 20 months of treatment
other_tests.changes_over_time: P1: Initial slight improvement, then slight deterioration. P2: Initial improvement, then slight deterioration.

other_tests.date: Before treatment (T0) and after 20 months of treatment

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Autosomal recessive spastic ataxia of Charlevoix-Saguenay (ARSACS)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on clinical evaluation including spastic-ataxic gait, dysarthria, leg weakness, dysmetria, bilateral Babinski sign, pes cavus, and hammertoes. Brain MRI showed mild cerebellar atrophy and hypointense stripes in the pons. Electroneurography indicated severe sensorimotor axonal and demyelinating polyneuropathy. The presence of biallelic variants in the SACS gene confirmed the diagnosis.
